free_text: Main Reference.
Excitation functions of some neutron threshold
reactions on isotopes of molybdenum
Variable energy compact cyclotron CV 28
Quasi-monoenergetic neutron source.
Mo powder(99.98% pure) of natural isotopic composition
was packed in polyethylene tubes of 1.1 cm diameter
and 1.25 or 2.5 cm length. Ni and Fe monitor foils.
Irradiation for 4 to 6 h in each case.
Gamma-ray spectrometry.
The cross sections were calculated using the known
activation equation.
Neutron absorption factor was obtained from differences
between calculated and exp. ratios of flux densities
in front and at back of sample.
Ni and Fe monitor foils were placed in front as well
as at the back of the sample.
ENDF/B-IV,
dosimetry file.
Data used for
relative neutron flux densities calculation.
Reference for decay
data.
Error
analysis details.
Data taken from NP/A,435,43,1985.
-From the count rates of the activation products
obtained the contributions due to background neutrons,
which were produced via the interaction of deuterons
with structural materials of the gas target as well as
via the break-up of deuterons on deuterium gas, were
subtracted.
-Decay, gamma-ray intensity, efficiency of the detector
counting geometry, self-absorption, coincidence
losses, pile-up of gamma-rays, etc. were corrected.
Include both statistical and systematic errors.
Detailed description was taken from NSE 88(1984) 143.
Uncertainty in sample weight
Uncertainty in irradiation time
Error in half-life-dependent effects
Uncertainty in neutron source characteristics
Uncertainty in irradiation geometry
Uncertainty in correction for activity
induced by back-ground neutrons (gas in/out, breakup)
Uncertainty in correction for absorption and
scattering of neutrons in the sample
Error in excitation function of monitor react.
Uncertainty in determination of detector
counts 2 to 20 (statistics, pileup, coincidence
losses, geometry)
Uncertainty in absorption of gamma rays
in the sample
Uncertainty in detector efficiency
Uncertainty in chemical yield
Uncertainty in decay data
The minimum overall error was about 12%; in some cases
it was much higher due to low count rates (large
statistical errors) and/or due to high errors in
background neutron corrections for E > 6.0 MeV.
M.M. BIB information was added.
Free text was corrected to be "human-readable".
Dates were corrected for 4-digits year.
REACTION in Subents 002, 006 was corrected.
DATA-ERR was changed into ERR-T in according to text,
page 45. Subents 008, 009 were added.
SD: DECAY-DATA corrected in Subent 009.
Table 1 of main reference .
Nb-92 was changed into Nb-92-M in REACTION.
Decay data for Zr-89g were moved in Subent 008.
Measurements were performed after suitable decay time
so that the shorter-lived metastable state had
decayed out. This necessitated a small correction due
to the formation of some Nb-95 via the decay of Zr-95.
Contribution of Mo-96[(n,d)+(n,np)+(n,pn)]Nb-95 was
considered above 7 MeV and was found to be <0.5 %.
Special analyzing of gamma-ray spectrum was made over
the energy region of 730 to 790 keV since four
radioactive products Zr-95, Nb-95, Nb-98m, Mo-99 with
very different counts rates had to be analyzed.
Table 1 of main reference .
Contribution of Mo-97[(n,d)+(n,np)+(n,pn)]Nb-96 was
considered above 7 MeV and was found to be <0.5 %.
